item.title: Brookfield Sees Energy Deals Targeting Batteries Displacing Standalone Solar, Wind
item.description: Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. says contracts that pair clean energy generation with battery storage options are starting to replace pure renewables deals.

item.title: Trump Pulls Plug on Carolina Wind Project as Cancellations Mount
item.description: The Trump administration is canceling a lease held by Duke Energy Corp. off the coast of North Carolina as it expands its campaign to block new offshore wind developments.

item.title: Delhi to Ban New Gas-Powered Scooters, Trucks to Fight Pollution
item.description: India’s capital New Delhi will ban new petrol- and natural gas-fueled two-wheelers and small trucks from its roads over the next two years in one of the most aggressive moves yet by an Indian state to curb air pollution.

item.title: Paris Embraces District Cooling to Battle Deadly Heat
item.description: Europe is experiencing an early, intense heat wave.

item.title: Solar Boom Poses World Cup Test for Brazil’s Power Grid
item.description: When Brazil faces Japan in the World Cup early Monday afternoon, operators of the country’s power grid will have another concern in mind: keeping the system stable as millions of Brazilians step away from factories, offices and stores to gather in front of TVs.

item.title: After False Starts, Weather Trading Gains Traction as Renewables Grow
item.description: The expanding suite of contracts provides utilities, traders and generators of renewable power more options to hedge weather risk.

item.title: El Niño Threatens to Deepen Crisis for Brazilian Rice Farmers
item.description: A looming El Niño is threatening to deepen a financial crisis for rice farmers in southern Brazil, where years of extreme weather have already sent costs soaring and forced the country’s top producing region to slash future planting plans.

item.title: Extreme Heat Grips Eastern Europe After Records Fall in Germany
item.description: The deadly heat wave that’s set temperature records across western Europe for more than a week has shifted east to scorch Hungary, Romania and the Balkans.

item.title: America’s Biggest Wind Farm Arrives Just as Industry Heads for Declines
item.description: The pace of onshore turbine installations is set to slow until 2030 as the renewables sector faces a myriad of headwinds.

item.title: How Paris Is Using the Seine to Try to Keep Cool in Scorching Heat
item.description: Chilled water and miles of pipes create eco-friendly air conditioning for the city

item.title: Shark Attacks Prompt Daily Drone Monitoring of Sydney Beaches
item.description: Sydney’s beaches will have daily dawn-to-dusk drone monitoring for sharks starting in July, following a spate of attacks off the Australian coast.

item.title: How AI-Powered Public Comments Are Entering US Climate Politics
item.description: Officials have been confounded by the new phenomenon.

item.title: Europe’s Heat Wave Raises the Cost of Waiting to Adapt
item.description: The early, intense heat wave sweeping across Europe is testing which systems can still cope — and forcing difficult questions about whether to endure more extreme temperatures or invest billions in adapting to a hotter future.

item.title: US Appeals Court Rejects Trump EPA Bid to Roll Back Soot Pollution Limit
item.description: In a blow to the Trump administration’s deregulatory agenda, a federal appeals court on Friday rejected the Environmental Protection Agency’s attempt to roll back soot pollution standards set in 2024.

item.title: Why Is Europe Warming Twice as Fast as the World Average?
item.description: Europe is contending with a new climate reality as extreme heat is no longer a rare occurrence.

item.title: LA Palisades Fire Jury Fails to Reach Verdict in Arson Trial
item.description: A jury failed to reach a verdict in the arson case over the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history.

item.title: China Three Gorges Is Said In Talks to Buy German Wind Assets
item.description: China Three Gorges Corp. is in talks to buy a German wind portfolio from NEAG Norddeutsche Energie AG, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Europe’s Record-Breaking Heat Wave Prompts Questions About How to Adapt
item.description: The fastest-warming continent is facing tough questions about how to adapt.

item.title: Earthquake Hits Close to Japan’s Mt Fuji, No Tsunami Expected
item.description: An earthquake of magnitude 5.6 hit Yamanashi prefecture west of Tokyo late on Friday, the Japan Meteorological Agency said.

item.title: One of Spain’s Biggest Banks Is Adjusting Client Pricing in Response to Global Warming
item.description: BBVA SA is starting to adjust what it charges clients for loans, based on how exposed they are to the physical effects of global warming.

item.title: Germany Joins Push to Change EU Methane Rules on Supply Concerns
item.description: Germany backed an initiative by a group of European Union governments to amend the bloc’s methane-emissions regulation, arguing that some provisions could threaten energy security.

item.title: Europe’s Brutal Heat Wave Is Set to Spread East This Weekend
item.description: Record-breaking heat that’s blanketed western Europe for more than a week is set to sweep east this weekend, with forecasts for searing temperatures across Germany, Poland, Austria and the Czech Republic.

item.title: Extreme Heat Adds Pressure to Food Supplies Already Hit by War
item.description: The scorching heat sweeping Europe has parched soils, distressed livestock and is keeping farmers away from fields, superseding the Iran war as the greatest challenge to food supplies.

item.title: As Europe Bakes, Heatwaves Are Already Biting Into Paychecks
item.description: Extreme heat and drought have cut average household incomes across the continent, putting millions at risk of poverty, according to a new study.

item.title: Europe’s Heat Wave Is the Worst on Record, Researchers Find
item.description: High night-time temperatures and humidity made June’s weather particularly dangerous, scientists said.

item.title: Weak Monsoon Emerges as India’s Next Economic Worry, After Oil
item.description: A weak monsoon is emerging as India’s next inflation risk, with a developing El Niño threatening to curb rainfall and push up food prices just as an easing oil relieves broader price pressures.

item.title: Heat Waves Spur UK Lawmakers to Seek Government Response Plan
item.description: British lawmakers pressed the government on its plans to protect citizens from scorching weather as the UK experiences its second heat wave this year and the hottest June temperatures on record.

item.title: Screams Echo as Venezuela Searches for Survivors After Quakes
item.description: Screams and groans echoed from beneath piles of concrete and bricks during the early morning hours on Thursday as residents searched for survivors in the coastal city of Catia La Mar, where rescue teams had yet to reach several collapsed buildings nearly 10 hours after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela.

item.title: Hedge Fund Eyes Crop Disruption as Super El Niño Looms
item.description: Moreton Capital Partners is shifting focus to extreme weather.

item.title: Why the Trump Administration Is Defending Musk’s xAI Data Centers in Court
item.description: The government’s bid to shield the AI company from a pollution lawsuit is raising concerns that citizens could lose a longstanding tool for enforcing environmental rules.
